findings.evidence: `rg -n "orbitQuotientEquivBase.*symm|symm.*orbitQuotientEquivBase|orbitQuotientEquivBase_mk"` finds only `orbitQuotientEquivBase_mk` at line 145; Mathlib's generic `Equiv.symm_apply_apply` does not expose the quotient representative form.
findings.fix: Add a `@[simp]` lemma such as `hreg.orbitQuotientEquivBase.symm (p e) = (Quotient.mk'' e : MulAction.orbitRel.Quotient (Deck p) E)`.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientEquivBase` has only the forward representative simp lemma; there is no characteristic inverse lemma identifying the class sent to a lifted base point.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "orbitQuotientToBase_injective_of_exists_apply_eq" code/TauCeti` shows the declaration at line 60 and its sole use at line 132.
findings.fix: Make this helper `private` or inline it into `IsRegular.orbitQuotientToBase_injective`; keep the public API at the `IsRegular` level.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientToBase_injective_of_exists_apply_eq` exports a non-regular helper whose only in-repo use is proving the regular injectivity lemma, widening the public surface beyond the roadmap target.

summary: The forward quotient-to-base API is mostly present, but the public interface is one-sided and also exposes a proof helper not needed by the named regular-cover target.
